paper_title: Diatom Assemblage Analysis in Freshwater Turtles

methods.description: Characterization of diatom assemblages using taxonomical and trait-based approaches to compare diversity among turtle species.

steps.description: The structure and composition of diatom assemblages were characterized using taxonomical and trait-based approaches.
steps.searchable_summary: characterize diatom assemblages using taxonomical and trait-based approaches

steps.description: Taxa richness and Simpson indices were used to compare diatom diversity among turtle species.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate taxa richness and Simpson indices for diatom diversity comparison

steps.description: Comparisons were made using pairwise T-tests adjusted by the Bonferroni correction.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pairwise T-tests with Bonferroni correction

steps.description: A generalized model was used to test the relationship between carapace area and diatom richness.
steps.searchable_summary: test relationship between carapace area and diatom richness using a generalized model

steps.description: A linear model was used to investigate the connection between carapace area and diatom diversity.
steps.searchable_summary: investigate connection between carapace area and diatom diversity using a linear model

steps.description: An ANOSIM analysis was used to compare assemblage composition among turtle species.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOSIM analysis to compare assemblage composition

steps.description: Non-Metrical multidimensional scaling ('nMDS') was used to explore how the taxonomical composition varied among turtle species.
steps.searchable_summary: use nMDS to explore taxonomical composition variation

steps.description: A posterior ANOSIM analysis was performed to evaluate if the groups of turtles drawn by the nMDS were different in terms of diatom species composition.
steps.searchable_summary: perform posterior ANOSIM analysis on nMDS groups

methods.description: Assessment of the functional composition of diatom assemblages using traits such as biovolume, life-form, and type of attachment.

steps.description: Trait data were obtained from existing trait-databases and complemented by observations in the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch trait data from databases and complement with lab observations

steps.description: Each species or genus was assigned to one trait category following Lange et al. (2016).
For each turtle species, the relative abundance of diatoms possessing a specific trait category was presented in the form of stacked bar graphs.
steps.searchable_summary: create stacked bar graphs for trait category abundance

methods.description: Procedure for sampling diatoms from turtle carapaces and processing them for analysis.

steps.description: For each species, three individuals were randomly selected for study.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly select three individuals per species for study

steps.description: The total area of each carapace was measured by tracing the carapace onto paper with a known weight per area, and then weighing the paper.
steps.searchable_summary: measure carapace area using paper tracing and weighing

steps.description: For each carapace, an area of approximately 19.2 cm² was brushed with a toothbrush.
steps.searchable_summary: brush carapace area with toothbrush to collect samples

steps.description: The samples thus obtained were preserved in 4% formalin.
In the laboratory, samples were oxidized using 30% hydrogen peroxide and mounted on slides using Naphrax®.
steps.searchable_summary: oxidize samples with hydrogen peroxide and mount on slides

steps.description: A total of 400 valves were counted for each sample, except those from P. unifilis and P. vogli, for which only 100 valves were counted.
Diatoms were identified to the lowest taxonomic-level possible following specialized keys.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatoms to lowest taxonomic level using keys
